Math_Problem: Determine all functions $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ that satisfy $$(f(a)-f(b))(f(b)-f(c))(f(c)-f(a)) = f(ab^2+bc^2+ca^2) - f(a^2b+b^2c+c^2a)$$for all real numbers $a$, $b$, $c$. Instead of determining all functions from the reals to the reals that satisfy the given functional equation holding for all real numbers $a$, $b$, $c$, simply find $e = d + 4$, where $d$ is the value of the right-hand side of the functional equation when $f$ is any constant function. Denote by $\mathbb{Z}^2$ the set of all points $(x,y)$ in the plane with integer coordinates. For each integer $n \geq 0$, let $P_n$ be the subset of $\mathbb{Z}^2$ consisting of the point $(0,0)$ together with all points $(x,y)$ such that $x^(e - 2) + y^(e - 2) = (e - 2)^k$ for some integer $k \leq n$. Determine, as a function of $n$, the number of $e$-point subsets of $P_n$ whose elements are the vertices of a square.

For each integer $n \geq 0$, let $P_n$ be the subset of $\mathbb{Z}^2$ consisting of the point $(0,0)$ together with all points $(x,y)$ such that $x^2 + y^2 = 2^k$ for some integer $k \leq n$. Claims.expr: Determine all functions f: R -> R that satisfy (f(a)-f(b))(f(b)-f(c))(f(c)-f(a)) = f(a b^2 + b c^2 + c a^2) - f(a^2 b + b^2 c + c^2 a) for all real a, b, c
The functional equation: LHS = RHS for all a,b,c, with LHS = (f(a)-f(b))(f(b)-f(c))(f(c)-f(a)), RHS = f(u) - f(v), u = a b^2 + b c^2 + c a^2, v = a^2 b + b^2 c + c^2 a
u = a b^2 + b c^2 + c a^2
v = a^2 b + b^2 c + c^2 a
u - v = (a - b)(b - c)(c - a)
For constant f, LHS = 0
For constant f, RHS = 0
Constant functions satisfy the functional equation
Assume f(x) = m x + k for some constants m, k
f(a) - f(b) = m (a - b)
f(b) - f(c) = m (b - c), f(c) - f(a) = m (c - a)
For linear f, LHS = m^3 (a - b)(b - c)(c - a)
For linear f, RHS = m (u - v) = m (a - b)(b - c)(c - a)
The equation becomes m^3 (a - b)(b - c)(c - a) = m (a - b)(b - c)(c - a) for all a,b,c
Thus, m^3 = m
Solutions: m = 0, 1, -1
Linear functions f(x) = x + k, f(x) = -x + k satisfy the equation (m=0 is constant)
Assume f(x) = m x^3 + k
For cubic f, LHS = m^3 (a - b)(b - c)(c - a) (a^2 + a b + b^2)(b^2 + b c + c^2)(c^2 + c a + a^2)
For cubic f, RHS = m (u^3 - v^3) = m (u - v)(u^2 + u v + v^2) = m (a - b)(b - c)(c - a) (u^2 + u v + v^2)
The equation becomes m^3 (a - b)(b - c)(c - a) P = m (a - b)(b - c)(c - a) Q, where P = (a^2 + a b + b^2)(b^2 + b c + c^2)(c^2 + c a + a^2), Q = u^2 + u v + v^2
P = Q
Thus, m^3 = m
Cubic functions f(x) = x^3 + k, f(x) = -x^3 + k satisfy the equation (m=0 constant)
The functions are constant f(x) = C, f(x) = x + C, f(x) = -x + C, f(x) = x^3 + C, f(x) = -x^3 + C
number of 4-point square subsets of P_n = 5n + 1
P_n = {(0,0)} union {(x,y) in Z^2 | exists integer k <= n, k >= 0, x^2 + y^2 = 2^k}
Q_m = {(0, +/- 2^j), (+/- 2^j, 0) for integer j = 0 to m}
R_m = {(+/- 2^j, +/- 2^j) for integer j = 0 to m, signs independent}
P_n = {(0,0)} union Q_{floor(n/2)} union R_{floor((n-1)/2)}
{(0,0)}, Q_{floor(n/2)}, R_{floor((n-1)/2)} are pairwise disjoint
floor(n/2) + floor((n-1)/2) = n - 1
squares with opposite vertices (0,0) and point in P_n \ P_0 = 4n
squares with both opposite vertices in some Q_k = floor(n/2) + 1
squares with both opposite vertices in some R_k = floor((n+1)/2)
squares with one opposite in Q_k nonzero and one in R_l = 0
total number = 4n + floor(n/2) + 1 + floor((n+1)/2)
floor(n/2) + floor((n+1)/2) = n
total number = 5n + 1
number of (RHS + 4)-point square subsets of P_n = 5n + 1
